events.summary: Sync on Claims Payment Observability Visualization

prep.summary: Align your upcoming claims visualization sync with the unified "Process Owner's Cockpit" concept—a dashboard framework you discussed with Faith Remley on 6-18 to synthesize disparate data for the "Pay Claim" process. Integrate these visualization needs with the business process health scorecard requirements—which Faith's team was tasked to define on 6-11—to support the upcoming target pilot. Drive the architecture toward near-real-time alerting rather than passive monitoring to ensure operational utility.
tasks: Confirm the status of Faith Remley's requirements documentation—specifically the definitions for transaction monitoring and process health scorecards.
Define the technical framework to ingest and synthesize disparate data from Celonis, Salesforce, and ServiceNow—the primary sources for the "Pay Claim" visualization.
Scope the target pilot by the end of Q3—focusing strictly on actionable, near-real-time alerting for SLA breaches.
Align the visualization strategy with Robbie's consolidated P&C business case—ensuring clear metric mapping to claims evolution benefits.
prep.title: Sync on Claims Payment Observability Visualization

prep.summary: No direct work notes exist for your last meeting with Sai—you must reconstruct those details during the sync. Your communications with the architecture team reveal critical operational bottlenecks—severe capacity burnout, unclear decision-making authority in the P&C space, and a preference for simple chatbot prototypes. For your investments list, you must review two high-stakes initiatives—the $15 million CMT telematics proof of concept and the parallel task-mining path with Skan.ai.
tasks: Address the missing documentation for your prior meeting with Sai—reconstruct recent alignments regarding process health and architecture standards.
Resolve the architecture team's capacity issues—engage with Deepak to establish clear technology decision-making accountability.
Finalize the chatbot prototype approach—re-use existing bill-pay code designs to bypass unnecessary tooling.
Evaluate the $15 million CMT telematics data output—ensure the integration with Iceberg preserves your future architectural autonomy.
Consolidate the P&C process mining business cases—align Claims, DNS, and Agency inputs for the upcoming pilot.
prep.title: Monthly Architecture Sync with Sai
